title: Safety Ops Chatbot (Construction)
problem: Construction professionals struggle to quickly find, interpret, and apply the correct safety procedures — OSHA standards, company policies, and incident records are in different systems.
solution: Multi-source RAG agent answers safety questions by searching OSHA standards, company safety policies, safety risk registers, and incident records — providing grounded, compliant answers with citations.
workflowSteps: Safety question submitted (optionally with uploaded photo or incident file)
Parallel retrieval across: OSHA safety standards, OSHA injury incident database, company safety risk register, safety health & environment policies
LLM synthesizes a compliant answer citing both regulatory and internal sources
User receives answer with source citations from each knowledge base consulted
keyInsights: Multi-source RAG where sources have different authority levels (OSHA regulation > company policy > incident record). The response must make the source hierarchy explicit so the reader knows what is regulatory vs. internal guidance.

relatedExams.why: Applications and Integration — multi-source knowledge base design

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart LR
  A([Safety Question]) --> B[Parallel Search]
  B --> C1[OSHA Standards - Regulatory Authority]
  B --> C2[Company Safety Policies - Internal Guidance]
  B --> C3[Incident Records - Historical Data]
  C1 & C2 & C3 --> D[Synthesize with Source Hierarchy]
  D --> E([Compliant Answer with Citations])
architectureNotes: Site workers query the chatbot via a mobile interface. RAG retrieves relevant procedures from the safety manual and incident database. Hazard-specific queries trigger additional context from the regulatory compliance database. Answers include direct citations; high-severity hazard queries simultaneously alert the site safety officer. All queries are logged for compliance audit trails.

failureModes.mode: Worker asks hazard question offline (no network on site)
failureModes.mitigation: Critical safety procedures cached locally on device; offline mode serves cached answers with clear staleness warning; sync on reconnect.

failureModes.mode: Chatbot gives wrong PPE recommendation for chemical exposure
failureModes.mitigation: Hazard queries routed to curated regulatory corpus only (SDS sheets + OSHA standards); no parametric model knowledge used for PPE recommendations; source citation required in every answer.

failureModes.mode: Safety officer not alerted for high-severity incident report
failureModes.mitigation: Severity classification is rule-based (keyword match on injury/fatality/fire/chemical release); PagerDuty integration fires immediately on match regardless of LLM response path.

scalingConsiderations: Site count: multi-site construction companies have 50–200 active projects; deploy shared corpus with project-specific addendum layer for site-specific procedures.
Query volume: 10–50 safety queries/day per active site; embedding corpus size manageable (<100K vectors for typical safety manual + incident database).
Regulatory update frequency: OSHA standards update infrequently; re-index quarterly or on published change notification.

integrations.note: Source of site procedures, PPE requirements, emergency protocols
Historical incident data for RAG; logs new incident reports
CFR Title 29, state OSHA standards, SDS sheets
Real-time alerts for high-severity hazard queries
